title: EPIC-SKELETON detection in tk: surface missing process ticks mechanically
description:
Skill docs now define the EPIC-SKELETON invariant (every runnable epic ends with a final-review tick then a close-out tick; see skills/ticks/SKILL.md Big picture + references/agent-runner.md Meta-work ticks). Today the runner pre-flight detects/repairs via prose + title matching. Make tk emit the signal instead:

- tk graph <epic> --json returns missing_process_ticks: ["final-review","close-out"] when the skeleton is incomplete
- Ideally structural rather than title-matching: a --role review|closeout flag on tk create, persisted on the tick
- Detect in tk, repair in skill — keeps CLI runner-neutral; no auto-scaffolding on -t epic

Rationale: mechanical signals beat prose; mirrors needs_planning:true and tk next action fields.
acceptance_criteria: tk graph --json includes missing_process_ticks for epics lacking the skeleton; role is stored structurally, not inferred from titles; covered by Go tests
closed_reason: Completed: structural role field (review|closeout) on ticks; --role on tk create/update; tk graph --json returns missing_process_ticks with human-output warning; role surfaces in graph wave tasks and tk next --json; schema + codegen regenerated; Go + UI tests green; skill docs updated to use the mechanical signal
